title: Phase B §2 real-model conformance nightly result
description: One nightly run of the Phase B §2 conformance gate. Written by .github/workflows/nightly_phase_b_gate.yml to tests/phase_b_gate/last_run.json, validated by tests/validate_artifacts.py on every PR, and read by the `atelier-conformance-status` binary. Records per-strategy `ConformanceSummary` rows + the aggregate `ConformanceStatus` verdict (green/yellow/red) against the `PHASE_B_CONFORMANCE_FLOOR` (PROVISIONAL 0.95 per spec §2). The 7-night calibration window per `tasks/phase_b_closeout.md` decision row #3 records-only for the first week; once `calibration_phase` flips to `false`, the workflow asserts against `max(0.95, observed_p5)` from the rolling 7-day window.

run_id.description: RFC 3339 timestamp the run completed at. Acts as the natural key for run-to-run comparison + rolling-window p5 computation.

git_sha.description: Commit the gate ran against. Short or full sha both accepted. v60.36 H6 — accepts mixed case so historic artifacts and `gh`-CLI-sourced output (whose case-normalisation isn't guaranteed across versions) validate without prior lowercase normalisation.

workflow_run_url.description: GitHub Actions run URL for debugging. Optional; missing for local runs.

all_passed.description: True iff the run's verdict is `green` OR `yellow` (the calibration phase keeps the nightly green even when below floor — see `calibration_phase`). `red` flips this to false.

calibration_phase.description: True during the first 7 nights of records-only operation. While true, the workflow does NOT assert against the floor — the artifact records the rate but `all_passed` only flips on red verdicts emanating from below-floor evidence (which the for_run lattice currently treats as Yellow during calibration). After 7 nights of green data, the maintainer flips this to false in the workflow + reruns; future evidence asserts at `max(0.95, observed_p5)`.

floor.description: Effective floor the run was compared against. Equal to PHASE_B_CONFORMANCE_FLOOR (0.95) during calibration; equal to max(0.95, observed_p5) once calibration completes. Optional — older artifacts may pre-date the field.

summaries.description: One row per emission strategy the model actually used. Order is the runner's emission order (NativeTool < JsonSentinel < RegexProse). An empty array is allowed in artifacts written when no conformance data was captured — the schema accepts it; the status binary surfaces it as `Yellow: no evidence`.

providers_tested.description: Provider strings (e.g. `anthropic:claude-haiku-4-5`, `openai-compat:gpt-4o-mini`) the run exercised. Optional in v1 so a pre-Track-B artifact (anthropic-only) is still valid.

ConformanceStatus.description: Stable wire labels from atelier_core::protocol_conformance::ConformanceStatus::wire_label(). Pinned by `conformance_status_wire_labels_are_stable`.

total_turns.description: Total envelope-parse attempts observed for this strategy during the run.

malformed_turns.description: Subset of total_turns where the envelope failed to parse. The runner's degradation policy fires on the rolling-window failure ratio (see DEFAULT_DEGRADATION_WINDOW / DEFAULT_DEGRADATION_THRESHOLD); the nightly artifact reports the run-level number.

rate.description: Success rate: (total_turns - malformed_turns) / total_turns. 0.0 when total_turns == 0 (the summaries() projection suppresses rows in that case).

verdict.description: Per-strategy verdict from ConformanceStatus::for_summary against the effective floor + min_window. Optional — older artifacts may pre-date the field.
